proposals.rationale: IL23R has one crystal structure (6WDQ) at 2.3A resolution showing the IL-23/IL-23R interface. The proposed epitope (A78-A80 and A112-A115) covers two HIGH-confidence interface residue clusters from validated_findings.json. VHH is the safest modality given the cytokine-receptor topology — flat-ish surface, no deep pocket. Caplacizumab and ozoralizumab scaffolds have strong PDB co-crystal evidence against similar cytokine receptors. Standard tier (5000 designs/scaffold) is appropriate for a moderate-novelty target.
key_risks: N91 N-linked glycan adjacent to proposed epitope; BoltzGen does not model glycans
ipSAE may underestimate cytokine-receptor interface quality due to extended interface
mitigation: Run Protenix 5-seed ensemble validation on top 10 designs to confirm interface
Cross-check designs against deglycosylated reference structure

proposals.rationale: The standard interface residues (A78-A80, A112-A115) are already covered by approved therapeutics like risankizumab. Targeting an unexplored region adjacent to the N91 glycan with a de novo helical bundle could yield a first-in-class binder with a differentiated mechanism. PXDesign supports de novo helical bundles natively. Even at 10% hit rate, this campaign validates whether the alt-epitope is druggable.
key_risks: N91 glycan steric occlusion of proposed epitope
De novo binders for cytokine receptors have lower precedent vs antibodies
Production tier requires 40h wall time on local GPU

proposals.rationale: Hedge across two competing hypotheses: (1) standard IL23R epitope is the right target (VHH canonical sub-run), (2) alt-epitope around N91 is druggable (VHH alt + de novo probe). If sub_001 fails, sub_002+sub_003 may still produce hits. Cross-aggregation by composite_score lets us compare apples-to-apples.
key_risks: Sub-run aggregation across modalities can be noisy
De novo probe (500 designs) may be underpowered
mitigation: Use modality-stratified ranking before cross-run aggregation
Treat sub_003 as exploratory signal, not statistical result

candidates.scientific_rigor_justification: Cites 3 HIGH-confidence sources; epitope traces directly to validated_findings.json findings 1, 3, 5.
candidates.feasibility_justification: Standard nanobody-anything protocol with default scaffolds; 8h on local GPU is well within budget.
candidates.innovation_justification: Conventional VHH approach against well-characterized epitope; no novel modality or scaffold.
candidates.risk_adjusted_confidence_justification: Stated confidence 0.78; both risks have explicit mitigations; penalty (1 - 0.15 * 0.4) = 0.94; 0.78 * 0.94 = 0.732.

candidates.scientific_rigor_justification: Cites 2 HIGH and 1 MEDIUM source; one sub-hypothesis (alt-epitope) relies on inference.
candidates.feasibility_justification: Three parallel sub-runs add coordination overhead; aggregation across modalities is non-trivial.
candidates.innovation_justification: Hedging across modality and epitope is a creative approach; de novo probe explores untested space.
candidates.risk_adjusted_confidence_justification: Stated confidence 0.65; risks acknowledged with partial mitigation; modality-stratified ranking is sound but unproven for this target class.

candidates.scientific_rigor_justification: Cites only 1 source (src_009, MEDIUM confidence); epitope choice is speculative.
candidates.feasibility_justification: Production tier (20000 designs) on local GPU is 40h — tight but possible; custom helical bundles are supported by PXDesign.
candidates.innovation_justification: Novel modality (de novo) + novel epitope; clear first-in-class differentiation.
candidates.risk_adjusted_confidence_justification: Stated 0.55 confidence; 3 risks with only 1 mitigation = 2 unmitigated; 0.55 * (1 - 0.15*2) = 0.385, raised slightly for partial coverage.

ranking.rationale: Conservative wins on the strength of scientific rigor (0.85) and feasibility (0.90). The margin over Diverse (0.0525) is just above the tie-break threshold of 0.05, so no merge is forced — but the orchestrator should still consider the dissenting recommendations below. Aggressive is clearly third due to thin source coverage and high unmitigated risk count.
ranking.dissenting_notes: The Aggressive proposal raised a real concern about the N91 N-glycan that the Conservative proposal addresses only as a downstream Protenix check. Strengthening this in the campaign_config by enabling cross_check_glycan_clash before screening would close the gap.
merged_recommendations: Add 'cross_check_glycan_clash: true' to campaign_config.yaml screening block
If run_001 hit rate < 20%, automatically queue a run_002 using Diverse sub_002 epitope (A85-A90)
Reserve 1500 designs from the Conservative budget envelope as a contingency for the alt-epitope follow-up
